claim: The observed 31/23 alignment is certified as a shared numeric boundary between the D20 intrinsic hex metric, the 31-element sector33 height attachment, the 23-dimensional Wu syzygy profile, and the externally marked hexacode/Golay endpoint. The explicit 31 -> 24 -> 23 Golay-Hamming correspondence morphism remains open and is not promoted to a theorem here.

definition.certified_open_boundary: The probe is successful only because it records both the positive alignments and the obstruction/canonicity seams that prevent a direct Golay theorem.
definition.morphism_under_test: a canonical delete/contract/select functor from the 31-element sector33 matroid to the 24-coordinate Wu/Golay frame, compatible with the six-column hexacode selector, followed by a typed puncture to 23 coordinates

next_highest_yield_item: Try the explicit minor/puncture search: enumerate natural delete/contract sets from the sector33 dual cocircuit and e33 wall data, then compare any induced 24-coordinate binary row space against the certified hexacode/Golay selector.

obstruction_boundary.hexacode_canonicity_reason: source_drop proved row-refined selector is not determined by column-pair/Wu/6j incidence; source_drop adjoins a row-pair/F4 hexacode selector and certifies the resulting Golay code.
obstruction_boundary.quadratic_selector_interpretation: The canonical 24-coordinate syzygy frame is real, but the natural quadratic/isotropic selectors tested here are obstructed. No nonzero symmetric or alternating form makes all 39 sector kernels totally isotropic. The support-incidence nullspace has Witt bound 10, so it cannot contain a rank-12 Type-II/Golay code. A Golay code therefore requires additional Wu/spin^h or external Golay marking data, not just the source_drop W24 kernel/support geometry.
obstruction_boundary.wu_golay_reason: The 23 linear syzygies form a basis of a 23-dimensional kernel of a 68-column multiplication map. This is numerically Golay-shaped, but it is not by itself a length-23 punctured-code coordinate system.
